input.content: You are a physiotherapy assistant. The user is doing an exercise and has raised a concern (pain, difficulty, boredom, etc.). Call get_exercises to fetch the full list, then suggest 2–3 suitable alternatives. For each suggestion include: name, why it suits the concern, and the source slug. Be concise — one short paragraph per suggestion.

Current exercise slug: ex_0016
User concern: Hip pain

output.content:
Here are a few alternative exercises you could try, keeping your hip pain in mind:

1.  **Exercise HAB3. Side lying leg raises**: This exercise specifically targets the muscles around your hip (abductors) in a non-weight-bearing position, which can help strengthen them without putting direct stress on the painful area. Source slug: ex_0060
2.  **Exercise HG1. Bridging**: This exercise strengthens your glutes and hamstrings, which are crucial for hip support and stability, while lying on your back, making it a low-impact option that avoids direct pressure on the hip joint. Source slug: ex_0037
3.  **Exercise HF1. Crook lying hip bends**: This exercise focuses on gentle hip flexion and control in a supported, lying position, which can help maintain hip mobility and strength without aggravating your pain. Source slug: ex_0063

Place the other end of the elastic around your opposite foot so you are standing on it.Stand and lean forward on your forearms over a table.ExerciseSlowly bend your arthritis knee, pulling against the

output.name: Exercise HG6. Seated knee flexion
output.intro: Starting positionSit upright in a chair. Move forward so you are sitting toward the front of the chair (so you have enough room to bend your knee under the chair).Place one end of an elastic band securely around a stable object (e.g. a heavy table leg). Loop the other end around the ankle of your ar

output.name: Exercise HG7. Hip extension with knee bent
output.intro: Starting positionStand and lean forward on forearms over a table or bench.ExerciseBend your arthritis knee to around 90 degrees.Push your foot backwards behind you so you are extending at your hip joint, while keeping your knee in the bent position.Hold for 5 seconds then slowly return to the starti

output.name: Exercise HG8. Hip extension with knee straight
output.intro: Starting positionStand and lean forward on forearms over a table or bench.Keep both legs straight.ExerciseLift your arthritis leg backwards behind you, keeping your knee straight. Lead with your heel, so you are extending at your hip joint. Keep the leg straight.Hold for 5 seconds then slowly return

output.name: Exercise HG9. Hip extension with knee straight - with elastic band
output.intro: Starting positionPlace one end of an elastic band securely around the ankle of your arthritis leg. Place the other end of the elastic around your opposite foot so you are standing on it.Stand and lean forward on your forearms over a table.ExerciseLift your arthritis leg backwards behind you, keeping

output.name: Exercise C1. Double calf raises
output.intro: Starting positionStand holding onto back of the chair.ExerciseSlowly rise up onto your toes.Hold for 5 seconds.Slowly lower.“Slowly up, hold, 2, 3, 4, 5, slowly down”.

output.name: Exercise C2. Single calf raises
output.intro: Starting positionStand holding onto back of the chair.Stand on your arthritis leg.ExerciseSlowly rise up onto your toes.Hold for 5 seconds.Slowly lower.“Slowly up, hold, 2, 3, 4, 5, slowly down”.

output.name: Exercise C3. Calf raises over edge of step
output.intro: Starting positionStand on a step with your heels over the edge.Hold onto the back of a chair for supportExerciseSlowly rise up onto your toes.Hold for 5 seconds.Slowly lower down, so your heels are below the step.“Slowly up, hold, 2, 3, 4, 5, slowly lower all the way down”.

output.name: Exercise C4. Single calf raises over edge of step
output.intro: Starting positionStand on your arthritis leg on a step with your heel over the edge.Hold onto the back of a chair for support.ExerciseSlowly rise up onto your toes.Hold for 5 seconds.Slowly lower down, so your heel is below the step.“Slowly up, hold, 2, 3, 4, 5, slowly lower all the way down”.

output.name: Exercise B1. Tandem stance
output.intro: Starting positionStand on a firm surface. Looking forwards focusing on a point on the wall.Use hand support (e.g. a chair) for balance if required.ExercisePlace one foot in front of the other so that feet make a straight line.Hold for 10 seconds.“Hold, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, relax”.Switch foot posi

output.name: Exercise B2. Forwards tap
output.intro: Starting positionStand on a firm surface.Use hand support (e.g. a chair) for balance if required.ExerciseSlowly tap forwards and back with the ‘tapping leg’ (non-arthritis leg) while balancing on the arthritis leg. Start with tapping just a few inches forwards and backwards and progress to larger ta

output.name: Exercise B3. Single leg balance
output.intro: Starting positionStand with your feet shoulder width apart.Stand close to a wall for support in case you over balance, if required.Use hand support (e.g. a chair) for balance, if required.ExerciseStand on single leg.Try to hold for 10 seconds.“Lift, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, relax”.Keep your weight on

output.name: Exercise HE1. Face-down-lying leg lift
output.intro: Starting positionLie on your tummy with your hands folded under your chin.Bend the knee of your arthritis leg, with your toes pulled up.ExerciseKeep your hips flat on the floor/firm bed.Push your heel towards the ceiling, lifting your leg off the bed, and squeezing your buttocks the whole time.Hold

output.name: Exercise HE1.2: 4-point-kneel leg lift
output.intro: Starting positionKneel on your hands and knees. Place your hands directly under your shoulders and your knees directly under your hip.Lift your tailbone in the air a tiny, tiny bit so there is a very small curve in your back but your back is almost straight.ExerciseKeeping the tummy drawn in, slowly

output.name: Single leg bridge
output.intro: Starting positionLie on your back with your knees bent and your feet flat on the floor/firm bed.Exercise1. Lift your non-study leg off the floor/bed. output.name: Exercise HE3. Hip thrust

output.name: Exercise F1. Partial  squats
output.intro: Starting positionStand up tall with your legs shoulder-width apart. Turn your feet slightly outwards.Stay safe: Hold onto a table or chair for balance.ExerciseBend at your hips and knees.Lower yourself down slightly, as if you were going to sit on a chair. Remember, when we sit down on a chair our b

output.name: Exercise F4. Backward step downs
output.intro: Starting positionStand on a step with your non-arthritis leg close to the edge of the step.ExerciseBending your arthritis leg slowly, sit your buttocks back and lean forward slightly.Make sure the knee on the arthritis leg side is not bending/drifting in towards the midline.Lightly touch the foot of

output.name: Exercise F5. Lunges
output.intro: Starting positionStand with your feet shoulder-width apart with your arms to the side or hands resting on your hips.ExerciseTake a large step forward with your non-arthritis leg, then lower your hips down, bending your hips and knees to about 90 degrees.Keep your upper body upright. Don’t let the kn

output.name: Exercise HAB3. Side lying leg raises
output.intro: Starting positionLie on your side on a firm surface, with your arthritis leg on top.You may bend the bottom leg and use your arms for support.The body and hips should be rolled forwards, about a ¼ turn forwards. This step is important; you should feel that you are rolling forwards slightly. This pos

output.name: Exercise HAB5. Hip hitch
output.intro: Starting positionStand side-on on a step, with your arthritis leg on the step and the non-arthritis leg hanging off the step. Hold onto a wall or handrail.ExerciseBend the knee on the arthritis leg very slightly.Lower the leg that’s hanging off the step by lowering that side of the pelvis down towar

output.name: Exercise HAB6. Bridge with banded abduction
output.intro: Starting positionLie on your back with your knees bent and your feet flat on the floor/firm bed. output.name: Exercise HF1. Crook lying hip bends
output.intro: Starting positionLay on a firm surface/bed, with both knees bent and feet resting flat on the bed.ExerciseThink about drawing your leg up into the hip joint and slowly raise your arthritis leg into the air.Slowly lower your arthritis leg down. output.name: Exercise HF1.2. Face up lying hip bends off edge of bed
output.intro: Starting positionLay on a firm surface/bed, with your arthritis leg hanging off the end of the bed.Bring the knee of your non-arthritis leg to your chest, and secure with your arms (please note the model in this photo has not used her arms, please try and do so).Stay safe: It is very important you m

output.name: Exercise HF2. Standing knee raises
output.intro: Starting positionStand tall with your legs shoulder width apart.Stay safe: Use a chair or table for balance.ExerciseStanding tall and strong, bend your arthritis leg up so that your thigh is parallel to the ground (or lower if discomfort is experienced).Slowly lower your leg back down. output.name: Exercise HER2: Hip aeroplanes

message_history.content:
Here are a few alternative exercises you could try, keeping your hip pain in mind:

1.  **Exercise HAB3. Side lying leg raises**: This exercise specifically targets the muscles around your hip (abductors) in a non-weight-bearing position, which can help strengthen them without putting direct stress on the painful area. Source slug: ex_0060
2.  **Exercise HG1. Bridging**: This exercise strengthens your glutes and hamstrings, which are crucial for hip support and stability, while lying on your back, making it a low-impact option that avoids direct pressure on the hip joint. Source slug: ex_0037
3.  **Exercise HF1. Crook lying hip bends**: This exercise focuses on gentle hip flexion and control in a supported, lying position, which can help maintain hip mobility and strength without aggravating your pain. Source slug: ex_0063
